---
title: 'Half-Price Books Fort Worth, Texas Hwy 377'
date: '2014-02-10T01:36:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'LIFE in Review (s)'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '3521495563097529895'
blogger_filename: '/2014/02/half-price-books-fort-worth-texas-hwy.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/LIFE in Review (s)/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Enjoy'
  - 'Discover'
---

<h2 style="text-align: center;"><a href="http://www.hpb.com/" target="_blank">Half-Price Books</a></h2><h3 style="text-align: center;"><a href="http://www.yelp.com/biz/half-price-books-records-magazines-fort-worth-3" target="_blank">Fort Worth, Texas Hwy 377</a></h3><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/img-20140209-121901-6067a322873f.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/img-20140209-121901-6067a322873f.jpg" height="400" width="298" /></a></div><br /><h4>Rating (5 Being the Best)</h4>4.5!<br /><h4>Overview</h4>So I went to Pei Wei on Hwy 377 just south of the Watauga Target. Not my usual <a href="http://www.lifeinfortworth.com/2014/01/pei-wei-asian-diner-southlake-texas.html" target="_blank">Pei Wei</a>, but just as good!<br /><br />While I'm waiting for my ToGo order I take a walk next door, two doors down. There is a place called Half Price Books. Honestly I really enjoy a good book store, but didn't think much of the name or sign and I've past it up several times before. Today I have time so I walk in. This place is just what a good used book store should be. It's FULL of books.<br /><br />I walk into the smell of old pages, shelves lined, and stuffed, with material from top to bottom. A cove for each section lines the walls with shelves through the middle of the store as well. I found everything from Mitt Romney's Autographed book, to all the For Dummies editions. Then I turn the corner and...<br /><br />It's not just books!<br /><br />They have Wii, PS3, and Xbox games, magazines, comic books. It was a pretty cool experience, and I'll most definitely be back!<br /><br /><h4>Cost</h4>Exactly what you'd expect. Some items were $50, some were $3. I picked up a For Dummies brand book that retails new for $20 they had it for $9.99.<br /><h4>Kids</h4>They had a whole area just for kids, it was pretty cool. If you are looking for more kids books for your library at home, stop by Half Price Books.<br /><br /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/img-20140209-121806-82591b075770.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/img-20140209-121806-82591b075770.jpg" height="320" width="239" /></a></div><br /><h4>Tips to enjoy it better...</h4>They also have locations all over DFW, not just this one.<br /><br />Check out their website: <a href="http://www.hpb.com/">http://www.hpb.com</a><br /><br />&nbsp;They also buy and sell all of this. So if you have old books, comics, etc, that you'd like to sell to them, bring it by.<br /><br /><h4>Final Thoughts</h4>What a cool place. I'll be back for all my book hunting needs.<br /><br /><br /><h4><a href="flickr: LIF Master Set" target="_blank">Flickr Photos</a></h4><div style="text-align: center;"><iframe align="center" frameborder="0" height="500" scrolling="no" src="http://www.flickr.com/slideShow/index.gne?group_id=&amp;user_id=106439236@N07&amp;set_id=72157640753329873&amp;tags=Food,Entertainment,FortWorth" width="500"></iframe><br /></div><div style="text-align: center;"><small>Created with&nbsp;<a href="http://www.admarket.se/" title="Admarket.se">Admarket's</a>&nbsp;<a href="http://flickrslidr.com/" title="flickrSLiDR">flickrSLiDR</a>.</small></div><br /><h4>As Seen On:</h4><div><h4>Flickr:&nbsp;<a href="http://www.flickr.com/photos/darrellwolfe/sets/72157639481468746/" target="_blank">LIF Master Set</a></h4></div><h4></h4><h4>Urban Spoon:&nbsp;<a href="http://www.urbanspoon.com/br/13/6359/life-in-fort-worth-dallas-fort-worth" target="_blank">Life In Fort Worth</a></h4><h4>Yelp:&nbsp;<a href="http://lifeinwortworth.yelp.com/" target="_blank">LifeInFortWorth.Yelp.Com</a></h4><br /><h4>Please comment below if you have anything to add, say, or correct.</h4><br /><b>Life In Fort Worth by&nbsp;</b><b><span style="font-size: x-small;">Darrell G. Wolfe</span></b><br /><b><span style="font-size: x-small;"><br /></span></b><b>Disclosure of Material Connection: Some of the links in the post above are â€œaffiliate links.â€ This means if you click on the link and purchase the item, I will receive an affiliate commission. I may also have received one or more of the products or services mentioned above for free in the hope that I would mention it on my blog. Regardless, I only recommend products or services I use personally and believe will add value to my readers. I am disclosing this in accordance with the Federal Trade Commissionâ€™s&nbsp;<a href="http://www.gpo.gov/fdsys/pkg/CFR-2003-title16-vol1/content-detail.html" target="_blank">16 CFR, Part 255</a>: â€œGuides Concerning the Use of Endorsements and Testimonials in Advertising.â€ A Big Thank You to&nbsp;<a href="http://michaelhyatt.com/five-ways-to-comply-with-the-new-ftc-guidelines-for-bloggers.html" target="_blank">Michael Hyatt</a>&nbsp;for helping us Bloggers with these new legal disclosure requirements.&nbsp;</b><br /><h4>Blog:</h4><ul><li><a href="http://www.lifeinfortworth.com/" target="_blank">www.LifeInFortWorth.com</a></li><li><a href="https://www.facebook.com/LifeInFortWorth" target="_blank">Facebook.Com/LifeInFortWorth</a></li><li><a href="http://twitter.com/DarrellWolfe" target="_blank">Twitter.Com/DarrellWolfe</a></li></ul><h4>Connect:</h4><div style="clear: both; float: left; padding-bottom: 10px;"><a href="https://www.facebook.com/DarrellGWolfe" imageanchor="1"><img alt="Follow me on Facebook" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/facebook-icon-436ed0b74b58.png" style="border: 0px; height: 32px; margin-left: 5px; margin-right: 5px; width: 32px;" />&nbsp;</a><a href="https://twitter.com/DarrellWolfe"><img alt="Follow  &lt;b&gt;YOUR-TWITTER-ACCOUNT-NAME&lt;/b&gt; on Twitter" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/twitter-logo-0d130abda9d2.png" style="border: 0px; height: 33px; margin-left: 5px; margin-right: 5px; width: 33px;" />&nbsp;</a><a href="http://www.youtube.com/user/darrellgwolfe"><img alt="Follow YOUR-CHANNEL-ID on YouTube" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/youtube-icon-df42b17a5900.jpg" style="border: 0px; height: 33px; margin-left: 5px; margin-right: 5px; width: 33px;" />&nbsp;</a><a href="http://feeds.feedburner.com/TexasLivingInfo" rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml"><img alt="RSS subscription icon" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/rss-logo-d57cc3305733.jpg" style="border: 0px; height: 33px; margin-left: 5px; margin-right: 5px; width: 33px;" />&nbsp;</a>&nbsp;&nbsp; </div><br /><br /><h4><b>What is YOUR favorite place to go? Comment Below!</b></h4>
